steps.title: Find what the text says critics are skeptical about
steps.content: The passage states: "it's too early to tell if they can thrive in this unusual setting." "They" refers to the trees cultivated for the project. Critics doubt the trees — and by extension the plant life — can survive on the building's balconies.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Some essential aspects of Bosco Verticale's design are difficult to adapt to locations other than Milan.’: The text does not discuss adapting the design to other cities; critics focus solely on whether the current trees can thrive. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The plant life on Bosco Verticale ended up being less varied than Boeri had envisioned it would be.’: The text mentions no comparison between Boeri's vision and what was planted; critics question survival, not variety. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The construction of Bosco Verticale was no less environmentally damaging than the construction of more conventional buildings is.’: Construction impact is never mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It is unclear whether Bosco Verticale can support the plant life included in its design.’: Directly restates the critics' concern that it is too early to tell if the trees can thrive in the building's unusual setting. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
